sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.39 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: A group is said to be$X$-critical if it does not belong to$X$but all its proper subgroups belong to~$X$. Let$F$be a soluble hereditary formation of finite groups for which all$F$-critical groups are either groups of prime order or$U$-critical groups, where$U$is the formation of all supersoluble finite groups. Must$F$be a saturated formation?

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
